zh.lifeSummary: Ann Gillespie於大約1345年出生在英國藍卡爾蘭卡斯特。她與John de Rixton結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1413年在她的家鄉享年69歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Ann Gillespie于大约1345年出生在英国藍卡爾蘭卡斯特。她与John de Rixton结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1413年在她的家乡享年69岁。

nameMeanings.name: Gillespie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and Irish (Donegal): shortened Anglicized form of Scottish Gaelic Mac Gille Easbuig, borrowed into Irish as Mac Giolla Easpaig, a patronymic from a personal name meaning ‘servant of the bishop’.

variants.name: Bishop
meanings: English: from Middle English bissop, biscop, Old English bisc(e)op ‘bishop’, which comes via Latin from Greek episkopos ‘overseer’. The Greek word was adopted early in the Christian era as a title for an overseer of a local community of Christians, and has yielded cognates in every European language: French évêque, Italian vescovo, Spanish obispo, Russian yepiskop, German Bischof, etc. The word came to be applied as a surname for a variety of reasons, among them a supposed resemblance in bearing or appearance to a bishop, and selection as the ‘boy bishop’ on Saint Nicholas's Feast Day. In some instances the surname is from the rare Middle English (Old English) personal name Biscop ‘bishop’. As an Irish surname it is adopted for Mac Giolla Easpaig, meaning ‘servant of the bishop’ (see Gillespie ). In North America, this surname has absorbed, by assimilation and translation, at least some of continental European cognates, e.g. German Bischoff , Polish, Rusyn, Czech, and Slovak Biskup , Slovenian Škof (see Skoff ).

variants.name: Gillispie
meanings: Scottish (Perthshire) and Irish: variant of   Gillespie  .

variants.name: Gillaspy
meanings: Scottish and Irish: variant of   Gillespie  . This form of the surname is found mainly in Lancashire, England.

variants.name: Clasby
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Bridie.
Irish (Galway): Anglicized shortened form of   Gillespie  .
English: habitational name from Claxby Pluckacre in Lincolnshire, formed with the Old Norse (Danish) personal name Klak (see Clack ) + Old Norse býr ‘farm, settlement’.

variants.name: Espy
meanings: Scottish (western Scotland): from a shortened form of   Gillespie  .

French: topographic name for someone living near a prominent thorn bush or in an area characterized by such vegetation, from Old French espine (from Latin spina). Occasionally the name may derive from the same word used in a transferred sense of the crest or ridge of a hill.
In some cases also an altered form of German   Espey  .

variants.name: Gillaspie
meanings: Scottish and Irish: variant of   Gillespie  . This surname is now rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Glasby
meanings: Scottish and northern English: shortened form of   Gillespie  .
English (Lincolnshire): perhaps a variant of   Clasby  .

English (Hampshire): habitational name, a shortened form of Glastonbury, from the place in Somerset named in Anglo-Saxon times as ‘the fortified place (Old English burg) of the Glæstingas’ (the people of Glastonia, a Celtic name probably meaning ‘woad place’).

variants.name: Espie
meanings: Scottish: from a shortened form of   Gillespie  .

variants.name: Gallaspy
meanings: Scottish and Irish: variant of   Gillespie  .

variants.name: Glaspey
meanings: Irish or Scottish: shortened form of   Gillespie  .

nameMeanings.name: Ann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of Anne . In the U.S. Ann is the more frequent spelling.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Ann Gillespie was born about 1345, in Lancaster, Lancashire, England. She married John de Rixton. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in 1413, in her hometown, at the age of 69.

fr.lifeSummary: Ann Gillespie est née environ 1345, à Lancaster, Lancashire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé John de Rixton. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1413, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 69 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Ann Gillespie wurde um 1345 in Lancaster, Lancashire, England geboren. Sie hat John de Rixton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1413 im Alter von 69 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Ann Gillespie è nata il circa 1345 nel Lancaster, Lancashire, England. Lei sposato John de Rixton. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1413, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 69 anni.

ja.Lancaster, Lancashire, England: イギリスランカシュアイアランカスター
ja.lifeSummary: Ann Gillespieは約1345年にイギリスランカシュアイアランカスターで生まれた。John de Rixtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1413年に故郷で69歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Ann Gillespie(은)는 대략 1345년 영국 랭카실르 랭커스터에서 태어났다. 그녀는 John de Rixton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1413년 그녀의 고향에서 69세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Ann Gillespie nasceu aproximadamente 1345, em Lancaster, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com John de Rixton. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1413, em sua cidade natal, com 69 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Ann Gillespie родилась около 1345 г. в местности ланкастир, ланкашир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John de Rixton. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1413 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 69 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Ann Gillespie nació aproximadamente 1345, en Lancaster, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Se casó con John de Rixton. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1413, en su pueblo, a la edad de 69 años.
